recalls.Description:
The recall affects specific Model Year 2025 Spartan SRT-XD and KGZ-XD zero radius turn riding lawn mowers, identified by model numbers RSKXD161994VO, RSKXD172994VO, RSSXD161994VO, RSSXD161999KE, RSKXD172994VO, and RSSXD172999KE. These mowers feature a gray, black, and orange design with the 'Spartan' logo prominently displayed on the front. The model and serial numbers can be found on a metal plate attached to the mower's frame near the operator's feet.

The issue arises from the potential incorrect installation of the steering arm dampers, which may lead to an unexpected bouncing motion and a loss of control while operating the mower, creating a crash hazard. It is crucial for consumers to cease using these mowers immediately and seek assistance from an authorized Spartan dealer for inspection and necessary repairs.
recalls.Title: Recall of Spartan SRT-XD and KGZ-XD Riding Mowers Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please contact Spartan Mowers at 855-307-0933, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at service@spartanmowers.com or visit their website at http://www.joinspartannation.com and click on 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the page. For detailed recall information, visit https://joinspartannation.com/recall-information/spartan-srt-xd-and-kgz-xd-damper-recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Spartan Riding Lawn Mower (SRT-XD)
Recalled Spartan Riding Lawn Mower (KGZ-XD)
Recalled Spartan Riding Lawn Mower Model Number and Serial Number Plate
Recalled Spartan Riding Lawn Mower Model Number and Serial Number Plate

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the mower bouncing unexpectedly while in use, including one report of a minor injury involving scrapes and bruises.

Manufacturers.Name: Intimidator LLC d/b/a/Spartan Mowers and UTVs, Batesville, Arkansas

Retailers.Name: Available at Spartan dealers nationwide from February 2025 to October 2025, priced between $16,400 and $19,700.

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to incorrect installation of steering arm dampers

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair of steering arm dampers by authorized Spartan dealers

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall for Spartan SRT-XD and KGZ-XD riding mowers due to crash hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: STIHL has issued a recall for the BR 800 Magnum backpack blowers, specifically the X and C-E models, due to a potential laceration hazard caused by the fan wheel. These blowers are characterized by their gray and orange design, with 'STIHL,' 'BR 800,' and either 'X' or 'C-E' printed on the back. The affected units have serial numbers ranging from 546515117 to 547916107, which can be found on a label on the blower tube.
recalls.Title: STIHL BR 800 Backpack Blowers Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact STIHL Inc. at 800-233-4729 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information is available online at www.stihlusa.com/safety/recalls/br800 or by visiting www.stihlusa.com and navigating to 'Recalls & Notices' under the Information section, then selecting 'BR 800 Recall.'

Images.Caption: Recalled STIHL BR 800 X Magnum Backpack Blower
Recalled STIHL BR 800 C-E Magnum Backpack Blower
Location of Serial Number
Location of Serial Number

Manufacturers.Name: STIHL Incorporated, of Virginia Beach, Virginia

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized STIHL independent dealers across the country from May 2025 to September 2025, priced around $600 for the BR 800 X Magnum and approximately $700 for the BR 800 C-E Magnum.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard from breaking fan wheel

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blower and take it to an authorized STIHL dealer for a free inspection and fan wheel replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: STIHL recalls BR 800 backpack blowers due to a laceration hazard. Check if your model is affected and learn how to get it inspected.

recalls.Description: DR Power is recalling its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs, which are used with various DR Power battery-operated products like lawnmowers, trimmers, and snow throwers. The recall includes standalone battery packs with model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs that come with outdoor equipment models 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. The model number can be found on the battery pack's label, and the LiPRO and DR Power logos are displayed on the pack's front and side panels. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled batteries in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire hazard potential. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 5.0 Ah Lithium-Ion Battery Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Model 414240 Battery Pack (5 Ah)
DR Power Model 524340, DR Trimmer Mower
DR Power Model SB11022XEN, DR Pulse 62V Snow Thrower
DR Power Model T4X3062XEN, DR Trimmer Pulse
DR Power Model CE75021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21"
DR Power Model CE77021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21" SP

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents of fire and/or overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo New Team Import & Export Co. Ltd. ("Lera"), of China

Retailers.Name: Available at home improvement and hardware stores nationwide, as well as online at DRPower.com and CountryHomeProducts.com from April 2018 through July 2024, priced between $300 and $700 for outdoor equipment including battery packs, and $250 for individual battery packs.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential short-circuiting

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled battery packs and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction is required via two photographs uploaded on the recall website.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery packs due to fire and burn hazards. Contact for refunds and proper disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: DR Power Equipment is recalling its Tow Behind Field and Brush Mowers due to a potential laceration risk. The affected mowers, which feature a black and orange design, were manufactured between April 1, 2024, and August 20, 2024. Each mower has a model and serial number displayed on a white label located on the front frame. The manufacture date can be found on the serial number label on the rear frame, formatted as YYYYMMDD. The recall includes specific models such as the DR BRUSH MOWER PREMIER 44T, DR BRUSH MOWER PRO 44T, DR BRUSH MOWER PRO XL44T, and DR BRUSH MOWER PRO MAX52T.
recalls.Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Tow Behind Field & Brush Mowers Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to DR Power at 800-458-4362 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recalls@DRPower.com. More information is available online at http://drpower.com/recalls or by visiting www.drpower.com and selecting 'Product Recalls.'

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Equipment Tow Behind Field and Brush Mower

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of blade carrier detachments. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: DR Power Equipment, of South Burlington, Vermont, a subsidiary of Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide from January 2018 through July 2019

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to potential detachment of the blade assembly

Remedies.Name: Free repair of the blade carrier assembly at the dealer's location

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls Tow Behind Field & Brush Mowers due to laceration risks. Contact DR Power for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Doosan Bobcat North America has announced a recall of Bobcat and Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck attachments. These mower decks are entirely black and feature the label "AirFX" along with model identifiers "72" or "61". The recall includes specific model numbers, which can be found on the right-side frame rail at the rear of the attachment. The affected models are 7526575 (72" AirFX), 7526579 (61" AirFX), 7449223 (61" AirFX Finish Mower), and 7447341 (72" AirFX Finish Mower).
recalls.Title: Doosan Bobcat North America Issues Recall for Bobcat & Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck Attachments Due to Potential Crush Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Doosan Bobcat North America at 800-743-4340, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information can also be found online at www.bobcat.com/na/en/parts-service/service/recalls or by visiting www.bobcat.com and selecting "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Bobcat AirFX Finish Mower Deck Attachment
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 552 (In addition, about 27 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bobcat & Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck attachment

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of five incidents where the locking pins were unable to be installed to secure the deck in the upright or tilted position. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Doosan Bobcat North America, of West Fargo, North Dakota

Retailers.Name: Available through Bobcat and Steiner dealers across the country from April 2023 to September 2024, priced between $5,200 and $5,500.

Hazards.Name: Inability to secure mower decks in upright or tilted positions, leading to crush hazards during maintenance.

Remedies.Name: Inspect locking pins for proper insertion; obtain free repair from Doosan Bobcat North America or a Bobcat dealer. Use two 1,500 lbs-rated straps for safety during service.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Doosan Bobcat recalls AirFX Finish Mower Deck attachments due to crush hazards. Check your model and contact for repairs.

recalls.Description: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment has issued a recall for specific RYOBI Brushless 21" Cordless Walk-Behind Self-Propelled Multi-Blade Push Mowers (40V HP) due to a potential fire hazard. These mowers feature a black cutting deck with gray top housing and a black bag labeled with "RYOBI." The recall affects models RY401014BTLUS, RY401014US, RY401140US, RY401015BTLUS, RY401015US, RY401150US, RY401140US-Y, RY401150US-Y, RY401020, and RY401200, with serial numbers ranging from KC21032D010001 to KC21327N999999, located inside the green mower housing.
recalls.Title: RYOBI Battery-Powered Mowers Recalled by TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact TTI at 800-597-9624 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, or visit https://ryobi.ryobitools.com/support/safety or https://www.ryobitools.com/ and select "Important Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: RYOBI Brushless 21" Cordless Walk-Behind Mowers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 217,500 (In addition, about 28,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RYOBI Mowers--Models RY401015BTLUS, RY401015US, RY401150US, RY401150US-Y, RY401020, and RY401200
Recalled RYOBI Mowers-Models RY401014BTLUS, RY401014US, RY401140US, RY401140US-Y (Canada)

Injuries.Name: TTI has received 97 reports of overheating while the mower was in use, including five reports of fires. Two minor burn injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Inc., of Anderson, South Carolina

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and Direct Tools Factory Outlet stores across the nation, as well as online at www.homedepot.com and www.directtoolsoutlet.com from February 2021 to January 2025, priced between $500 and $700.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating push-on connector inside the powerhead.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled mower and contact TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Inc. for guidance on disabling the mower and obtaining a complimentary replacement 21" walk-behind mower.

recalls.metaDescription: TTI recalls RYOBI Battery-Powered Mowers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact TTI for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall for specific John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mowers equipped with Kawasaki engines. The affected models include Z720E, Z730M, Z740R, and Z760R, with serial numbers ranging from 1TC720E****010001 to 1TC720E****080657 for the Z720E model, and similar ranges for the other models. These mowers may experience a failure in the voltage regulator, which can lead to overheating and present significant fire and burn hazards.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mowers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Deere & Company at 800-537-8233 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET on weekdays and 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET on Saturdays. More information is available online at www.deere.com by navigating to the "Recalls" section under "Parts & Service" or directly at https://www.deere.com/en/parts-and-service/recall-information/. Additionally, Kawasaki Motors Corp. USA can be contacted toll-free at 866-836-4463 during the same hours or online at www.kawasakienginesusa.com/product-recall.html.

Products.Name: John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mower Z720E

Images.Caption: Recalled John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mower

Injuries.Name: Kawasaki has received five reports of fires and 26 reports of melting and/or smoking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Manufacturing Corp. U.S.A. (engine manufacturer), of Maryville, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized John Deere dealers across the U.S. from October 2018 to September 2024, priced between $8,000 and $11,500.

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Free repair through authorized John Deere dealers

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mowers due to fire hazards. Immediate action is required for affected models.

recalls.Description: Chervon North America has announced a recall of SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries manufactured prior to May 1, 2021. These batteries, marked with "SKIL" and "PWRCORE 40," were sold individually under model number BY8708-00 and as part of kits with SKIL 40V Brushless Push Mowers and Self-Propelled Mowers. The recall affects batteries with specific manufacturing date codes ranging from November 2019 to April 2021, which can be found on the nameplate near the battery terminals.
recalls.Title: Chervon North America Issues Recall for SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Chervon North America at 833-476-5325 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email BY8708-00recall@na.chervongroup.com, or visit www.skil.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Battery, model BY8708-00
Nameplate located on housing of the recalled battery shows model number and manufacturing date code (first 3 digits of serial number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 100 reports of thermal incidents involving the batteries including overheating, melting, smoking and fire. These included eight reports of minor burns and/or smoke inhalation and 49 reports of related property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's and various hardware and home improvement stores nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Lowes.com from October 2019 through December 2024, priced around $170 for individual batteries and $400 for combination kits.

Importers.Name: Chervon North America Inc., of Naperville, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled batteries and contact Chervon for a free replacement or refund. Registration for the recall can be completed at www.skil.com/recalls, where Chervon will provide a collection kit for returning the recalled battery.

recalls.metaDescription: Chervon recalls SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a replacement or refund.

recalls.Description: Stanley Black & Decker has issued a recall for specific models of DeWALT Battery walk-behind mowers from the 2024 model year. The affected models include the 21" Push mowers (DCMWP234U2 and DCMWP600X2) and the 21" Self-Propelled mowers (DCMWSP256U2 and DCMWSP650Y2). These mowers are characterized by their black, gray, and yellow colors, with 'DeWALT' displayed prominently in yellow. The model number can be found on the rear door, while the date code, indicating the week and factory of manufacture, is laser-printed on the left side above the rear wheel. Only mowers produced between the date codes of 2023 32-58 and 2024 13-58 are included in this recall. Mowers made after March 2024 (2024 14-58) are not part of this issue.
recalls.Title: DeWALT Battery Walk-Behind Mowers Recalled by Stanley Black & Decker Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled mowers immediately if they have been exposed to water. For assistance, contact DeWALT at 800-990-6421 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.dewalt.com/push-mowers-recall or navigate to https://www.dewalt.com and select 'Safety Notices and Recalls' at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: DeWALT 21" Battery Push Mower
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 46,200 (In addition, about 9,600 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of mowers failing to shut off when the bail handle is released and one report of a mower starting without a key inserted. The incident in which a mower started without a key inserted resulted in a finger laceration for which no medical attention was required.

Manufacturers.Name: Stanley Black and Decker Inc., of New Britain, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Potential laceration hazard due to failure to shut off or unintended starting

Remedies.Name: Free repair available at authorized service centers

recalls.metaDescription: Stanley Black & Decker recalls DeWALT Battery walk-behind mowers due to laceration hazards. Check your model and contact for repairs.

recalls.Description: ECHO has issued a recall for two models of its 59.7cc gas-powered backpack blowers, specifically the PB-5810H and PB-5810T. These blowers, which are intended for both residential and commercial applications, feature a striking orange and black design with the 'ECHO' logo on the starter recoil housing. The model and serial numbers can be found on a label located near the right side of the blower's black fan housing. The affected serial numbers for the models are: PB-5810H (P61312001001 to P61312002590) and PB-5810T (P61212001001 to P61212004155).
recalls.Title: ECHO Recalls Gas-Powered Backpack Blowers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach ECHO at 800-432-3246 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email through their website at www.echo-usa.com/customer-support. More information can also be found at www.echo-usa.com/recall or by visiting www.echo-usa.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled ECHO Blower, Model Number PB-5810H
Recalled ECHO Blower, Model Number PB-5810T
Recalled ECHO Blower serial number location

Manufacturers.Name: ECHO Inc., of Lake Zurich, Illinois

Retailers.Name: Golden Eagle Distributing Company in Rocklin, California, and various independent dealers across the country, available from May 2024 through June 2024 for approximately $430.

Hazards.Name: Potential fuel line leak creating a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled blowers immediately and contact an authorized ECHO dealer at www.echo-usa.com/store-locator to arrange for the free installation of a new fuel line. ECHO will also reach out directly to known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: ECHO recalls gas-powered backpack blowers due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free repair and check if your model is affected.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall for specific engines used in various lawn and garden equipment brands, including Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota. The affected models include Bad Boy Mowers such as Rebel and Rogue series, BigDog's Stout MP, and several Bobcat models like ZT6000 and ZT7000. The engines in question are from model years 2021 to 2023 and may pose fire and burn hazards due to overheating. Consumers are urged to check the model and serial numbers located on the engine fan housing for details on whether their equipment is affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for Engines in Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota Equipment Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Kawasaki Motors Corp. USA at 866-836-4463, Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, or visit www.kawasakienginesusa.com/product-recall. Bad Boy Mowers owners should check https://badboycountry.com/kawasaki-recall. John Deere customers can call 800-537-8233 or visit www.deere.com for more information. For BigDog, Cub Cadet, and Hustler, contact 888-848-6038 or visit their respective recall pages. Kubota owners can call 888-458-2682 or check https://www.kubotausa.com/service-support/safety/safety-notices.

Products.Name: Kawasaki Engines for Bad Boy Mowers

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to engine overheating

Remedies.Name: Stop using the equipment immediately and contact the respective manufacturer or authorized dealer for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls engines in popular lawn and garden brands due to fire risks. Check if your equipment is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: DR Power Equipment is recalling several models of walk-behind leaf blowers and vacuums, as well as tow-behind leaf vacuums due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected models may have loose components that can detach during operation, posing a risk to users and nearby individuals. Consumers are advised to check their units against the provided model numbers to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: DR Power Equipment Recalls Leaf Blowers and Vacuums Due to Risk of Laceration
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to DR Power LLP at 877-221-4038 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Walk-Behind Leaf Blowers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 57,200 (In addition, about 956 were sold in Canada)

Recalled DR Power Equipment tow-behind leaf vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 22 reports of loose pieces being ejected from the units. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at independent power equipment retailers nationwide and online at drpower.com from September 2014 through January 2024, priced between $1,300 and $4,000.

Distributors.Name: DR Power Equipment, of South Burlington, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration from loose components

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls leaf blowers and vacuums due to laceration hazards. Check your model and contact for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Honda is expanding its recall to include HRN216 and HRX217K6 lawnmowers, along with GCV170/200 G5B engines used in pressure washers and replacement engines for these lawnmowers. The affected lawnmowers are identifiable by their red and black colors and the "HONDA" logo on the clipping collection bag. Model and serial numbers can be found on a label on the upper rear of the mower deck. The engines are mounted on various OEM frames, with serial numbers engraved on a flat area of the engine. Replacement engines are designed for the HRN216 and HRX217K6 models, with their serial numbers also engraved on the engine.
recalls.Title: Honda Expands Recall of Lawnmowers and Engines Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact American Honda Motor at 888-888-3139 from 9 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email via https://crmshonda.my.salesforce-sites.com/service/pew2c, or visit https://powerequipment.honda.com/ and click on "Recalls and Updates" at the bottom of the page, or access more information at https://cdn.powerequipment.honda.com/pe/pdf/Recalls/kp4.pdf.

Products.Name: Honda HRN216 Lawnmower
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 200 additional lawnmower replacement engines (About 391,800 Lawnmowers and Pressure Washers Engines were previously recalled in September 2023)

Images.Caption: Recalled Honda Lawnmower Replacement Engine
Recalled Honda HRN216 Lawnmower
Recalled Honda HRX217K6 Lawnmower

Injuries.Name: Honda has received 2,966 reports of incidents related to camshaft failures since the last recall, in addition to the 2,197 reported incidents in the September 2023 recall, including 38 reports of minor injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc., of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from starter rope retracting unexpectedly due to faulty camshafts.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled lawnmowers and pressure washers, and contact an authorized Honda dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Honda recalls lawnmowers and engines due to injury risks. Contact Honda for a free inspection and repair.
